scenario: Apex Telco processes 9000 events per day on Camunda 8.8 and stores a created field as a date and time value such as date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00") on the process instance. A modeller needs the documented FEEL call from the conversion functions page that extracts only the time component time("11:00:00") from that date and time value on the process variable.
question: Which call on the conversion functions page extracts the time component from date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")?

options.text: to time(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")); the docs document a to time() conversion function that ingests a date and time value and returns its time component as a FEEL time value on the conversion functions page

time part(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")); the docs document time part() as the FEEL conversion function that returns the time component of a date and time value on the conversion functions page
as time(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")); the docs document as time() as the FEEL conversion function that returns a time from a date and time value on the conversion functions page

time(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")); the docs document time(from: date and time): time as a signature of time() that extracts the time component from the given date and time value and show it returning time("11:00:00")

a.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document a to time() function; the function that extracts the time component from a date and time value is time(from: date and time).

b.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document a time part() function; the function that extracts the time component from a date and time value is time(from: date and time).

c.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document an as time() function; the function that returns a time from a date and time value is time(from: date and time).

d.text: Correct. The conversion functions page documents time(from: date and time): time as a signature of time() that extracts the time component from a date and time value, and shows time(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")) returning time("11:00:00").

explanation: The time() function has two signatures on the conversion functions page. time(from: string): time parses a time string, and time(from: date and time): time extracts the time component from a date and time value. The page shows time(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")) returning time("11:00:00").
